title: Writing - Part 1: Urban Development
description: Your class has been discussing the challenges of rapid urban growth and the best ways to make cities more sustainable.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the strategies in your notes. You should explain which strategy you think is most effective, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Cities need more parks and natural areas to improve air quality and residents' wellbeing.
Rising house prices are forcing low-income families out of city centres.
Good public transport can reduce traffic congestion and carbon emissions significantly.

structure.intro: Introduce the issue of urban development and state which two strategies you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first strategy and its potential benefits for city residents.
structure.body2: Discuss the second strategy and compare its impact to the first.
structure.conclusion: Summarise your argument and explain which strategy should be prioritised.

content.modelAnswer:
As cities around the world continue to grow, urban planners face the challenge of making them liveable, sustainable and fair for all residents. Two strategies that could help are investing in affordable housing and developing efficient public transport systems.

The shortage of affordable housing is one of the most pressing issues in many cities. When property prices rise beyond what ordinary workers can afford, communities become fragmented and inequality increases. Building more social housing and regulating rental markets would help ensure that people of all income levels can continue to live and contribute to urban life.

Improving public transport is equally important. When reliable, affordable transport is available, fewer people use private cars, reducing traffic and pollution. A well-connected city also enables residents to access jobs and services more easily, improving overall quality of life.

In my opinion, affordable housing should be the priority. Without secure housing, other improvements are of limited benefit to those who are displaced or struggling financially. Ensuring that everyone can afford to live in the city is the foundation on which all other sustainable development must be built.
